required_complexity_patterns.notes: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs
Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute
Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters)
Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell
Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute Required: Multiple controls sharing name attribute groups (checkbox/radio clusters) Required: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell Required: Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span Reference DOM (Table layout form): Form laid out as HTML table with labels in td cells Reference DOM (Radio styled as buttons): Native radios hidden; visible pill buttons styled via CSS class Reference DOM (Disabled decoy field): Disabled name field looks required but cannot be filled; active duplicate below Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: pill-yes-no, label-wraps-deep, duplicate-name-attrs, iframe-apply, aria-labelledby-hidden. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: sidebar + main split apply layout. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Table layout form; Radio styled as buttons; Disabled decoy field.
